title: Trigger intent, trigger attempt, peer start, and peer completion are separate operational states
memory_summary: The five-team operating model uses the word trigger for several non-equivalent events. A durable request in TRIGGER_REQUESTS.json records intent. A scheduler update or direct-trigger API response records an attempt accepted by a control plane. A peer run beginning is a separate observation. A peer producing committed evidence is completion. Future reports must not collapse these states into the claim that another team ran.

state_model.meaning: A durable request asks a peer team to run later.
state_model.minimum_evidence: append-only trigger request with target, reason, requester, and timestamp

state_model.meaning: A direct trigger or scheduler mutation returned success.
state_model.minimum_evidence: tool response identifying the target automation or run request

state_model.meaning: Independent runtime evidence shows the peer invocation began.
state_model.minimum_evidence: run identity, start timestamp, and target team identity

state_model.meaning: The peer produced a repository artifact bound to a commit.
state_model.minimum_evidence: commit SHA plus artifact path and owning team

state_model.meaning: The peer's acceptance criteria were checked through the strongest available route.
state_model.minimum_evidence: queue item, verification result, artifact identity, and explicit limits
does_not_establish: claim truth beyond the stated verification

records.title: Trigger is not a unitary event
records.summary: The coordination rule requires a trigger request for peers, while the trigger registry explicitly describes durable requests as a fallback when no direct peer-trigger API exists. This proves a request channel, not peer execution.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Find an authoritative project decision that defines a trigger request as execution evidence; absent that, retain the layered state model.

records.title: Report the highest evidenced trigger state only
records.summary: Team reports should say trigger intent recorded, trigger attempt accepted, peer run observed, peer output committed, or peer completion verified. They should not say a peer ran unless run evidence exists.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Apply the vocabulary across five subsequent team reports and audit for any remaining leap from scheduler acceptance to peer completion.

records.title: Scheduler mutation success is not peer completion
records.summary: Acceptance by a scheduling or request layer confirms only that the control-plane mutation or request was recorded. Execution and completion occur later and require separate evidence.
contradicts: any narrative that equates a successful reschedule or trigger-request append with a completed peer run
records.falsification_or_review_route: Bind scheduler acknowledgements to later run IDs and committed peer outputs; compare cases where accepted triggers never produce completion evidence.

records.title: No repository schema yet links trigger attempts to peer output commits
records.summary: Current queue and trigger artifacts do not provide a shared correlation identifier connecting requester, accepted trigger, peer run identity, resulting commit, and verification status.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Search for an existing correlation schema outside the inspected files; otherwise add a trigger_receipt_id to future request, run, queue-update, and evidence artifacts.

records.title: Canonical queue completion must remain evidence-bound
records.summary: A peer-trigger event may alter scheduling but cannot by itself change an owned queue item to completed. Completion remains tied to the owner's required evidence and append-only queue update.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Audit queue transitions for entries marked complete without owner-produced required evidence.

contradictions_resolved.apparent_contradiction: The scheduler accepted the update, therefore the peer team ran.
contradictions_resolved.resolution: Scheduler acceptance establishes trigger_attempt_accepted only. Peer execution requires an independently observed run; completion requires committed and verified output.

contradictions_resolved.apparent_contradiction: A trigger request exists in durable state, therefore the requested work is underway.
contradictions_resolved.resolution: The request establishes intent and discoverability. It does not establish that any runtime consumed it.

supersession_notes.reason: The qualified proof-layer rule now also applies to coordination claims: control-plane acceptance and execution evidence are separate proof layers.

next_action: Define a trigger receipt schema with one correlation identifier spanning requester, target, control-plane acknowledgement, peer run identity, output commit, verification result, and explicit terminal state.
